summary: Closed-unmerged PR that replaced nine fighter assembly stubs with C implementations: three Crazy Hand animation/grab helpers, one common fighter rebirth camera helper, and five Popo/Ice Climbers SpecialS/SpecialLw helpers. The PR also added substantial Claude/decomp automation documentation and scripts. The body claims `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and 100% objdiff matches, but there were no review comments and the PR was closed without merge.

key_files.role: Decompiled `ftCh_Slam_Anim`, `ftCh_TagRockPaper_Anim`, and `ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B670`; added needed common attack include and float externs.
Decompiled five Popo/Ice Climbers special-move functions including duplicated ground/air HiStart IASA logic and SpecialLw animation cleanup paths.
Split existing padding to expose `ftIceClimberAttributes.x80` and added `speciallw` motion vars with a bitfield at `fp+2344:0`.
Replaced unknown comment for `fn_80122D2C` with address-bearing prototype `/* 122D2C */`.
Decompiled `ftCo_Rebirth_Cam`, computing camera subject position from fighter data and camera helper output.
New helper CLI for overnight decomp workflow: ninja progress, stream monitoring, result parsing, token usage, stub filtering, asm extraction, and progress tracking.
Large new automation script for unattended decomp attempts.
Wrapper for finding stubs across the repo.
New macOS launchd scheduler for running overnight decomp automation.
New repository guidance for Claude Code including build commands, code style, naming conventions, matching tactics, and module abbreviations.

what_changed: Replaced `/// #ftCh_Slam_Anim` with C that exits Crazy Hand slam when boss-library conditions trigger or animation ends, zeroes `self_vel`, sets fighter flags, clears `x1A5C`, and transitions through `ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015A888`.
Replaced `/// #ftCh_TagRockPaper_Anim` with C that handles boss-library interruption via `ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015BC88` and changes to motion state `0x180` when animation frames are exhausted.

Replaced `/// #ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B670` with C that changes to motion state `0x151`, makes the fighter invisible, installs `ftCo_800DB464` as `accessory1_cb`, calls `ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 0x1FF)`, sets `x2220_b3`, and refreshes animation.

Replaced `ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_IASA` and `ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_IASA` stubs with matching duplicated logic: check `cmd_vars[0]`, clear it, compare `ABS(input.lstick.x)` against Ice Climber attribute `x80`, update facing, and rotate part Y by `M_PI_2 * facing_dir`.

Replaced `ftPp_SpecialLw_Enter` stub with state initialization for throw/cmd vars and `mv.pp.speciallw`, transitions into `ftPp_MS_SpecialLw`, refreshes animation, and installs `fn_80122D2C` in `accessory4_cb`.

Replaced `ftPp_SpecialLw_Anim` and `ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Anim` with frame-end cleanup that destroys effects if `fv.pp.x2230_b0` is set, clears callbacks, resets part rotation, then enters either `ft_8008A2BC` on ground or `ftCo_Fall_Enter` in air.
Replaced `/// #ftCo_Rebirth_Cam` with C that fills a `UnkFloat6_Camera`, updates `CmSubject` coordinates from fighter motion vars and scale, and calls `ftLib_800866DC`.
Exposed previously padded Popo attribute field `x80` and added a `speciallw` motion-var struct with `x0` and bitfield `x4_b0`.
Added automation/documentation files for Claude-driven decomp workflows, including stub selection, stream JSON result parsing, progress files, launchd scheduling, and log extraction.
smart_moves: Split struct padding only where needed instead of using ad-hoc pointer arithmetic: `u8 _7C[0x80 - 0x7C]; float x80;` made the Ice Climber threshold access explicit.
Modeled SpecialLw motion state with a named `mv.pp.speciallw` struct and bitfield, making `fp+2340` and `fp+2344:0` accesses reusable for future Popo work.
Used shared logic for ground and air HiStart IASA functions without inventing different behavior; both variants have the same command-variable gate, stick threshold, facing update, and Y-rotation.

Used existing fighter helpers and callbacks (`Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, `ftCommon_UpdateFacing`, `ftPartSetRotY`, `efLib_DestroyAll`, `ftCo_Fall_Enter`) rather than raw low-level equivalents.
Applied explicit matching aids visible in the diff, including `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, and `ABS(...)`, consistent with the newly added CLAUDE.md guidance.
Kept camera code tied to named runtime types (`UnkFloat6_Camera`, `CmSubject`, `GET_FIGHTER`) and existing camera/library helpers, preserving subsystem semantics.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function needs a field inside an unknown region, split the padding and name the field by offset instead of casting through raw bytes.
For fighter motion vars, add a small union member for the specific action being decompiled; this lets code access `fp->mv.pp.speciallw.x0` and `x4_b0` clearly while preserving offset evidence.
Duplicated ground/air action callbacks may genuinely compile from identical C bodies; do not over-factor if separate functions are required by the action table.
Animation callbacks commonly use `!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)` as the transition/cleanup gate; match cleanup order carefully when clearing effects and callbacks.
Callback assignment is often semantically important for matches and behavior, such as `accessory1_cb`, `accessory4_cb`, `death2_cb`, and `take_dmg_cb` in these fighter functions.
For small stack-sensitive fighter functions, `PAD_STACK(n)` can be the appropriate final matching adjustment when source logic is otherwise clean.
Closed-unmerged PRs with no review should be mined cautiously: test-plan claims are useful evidence, but lack merge/review means patterns should be reused only after revalidation.
naming_conventions: Fighter functions follow file/module prefixes such as `ftCh_`, `ftPp_`, and `ftCo_`.
Newly exposed unknown struct members keep offset-derived names such as `x80`, `x0`, and `x4_b0`.
Header prototypes can carry address comments, as shown by `/* 122D2C */ void fn_80122D2C(Fighter_GObj* gobj);`.
Character/module abbreviations from the added guidance include `Ch` for Crazy Hand style symbols in this file and `Pp` for Popo.
The added CLAUDE.md states repository style preferences: lowerCamelCase file names, UpperCamelCase function names after module prefixes, and hex-offset-prefixed struct members.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(16)` in the Popo HiStart IASA and SpecialLw animation functions and `PAD_STACK(8)` in `ftPp_SpecialLw_Enter` to satisfy stack layout.
Used `ABS(fp->input.lstick.x)` for the stick magnitude comparison pattern.
Used float literals and casts matching project style, including `0.0f`, `1.0f`, and `(float)(M_PI_2 * fp->facing_dir)`.

Kept a type-punned read in `ftCo_Rebirth_Cam`: `camera_box->x10.y = *(f32*) &fp->mv.co.common.x8 + spC.x0.x;`; this is evidence of a matching-sensitive representation that should be revisited if the underlying motion-var type becomes clearer.
Relied on existing motion-state constants where known (`ftPp_MS_SpecialLw`) and raw numeric motion IDs where names were apparently unavailable (`0x151`, `0x180`).
The PR body explicitly reports `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and all touched functions as 100% objdiff matches.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
PR state is CLOSED with `merged_at: null`; no provided evidence explains why it was closed unmerged.

follow_up_queries: Check whether these nine decompiled functions landed in a later PR or were superseded after PR #2276 closed unmerged.
Search later Popo/Ice Climbers PRs for refinements to `ftIceClimberAttributes.x80` and `ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw` naming.
Search for later cleanup of the type-punned `fp->mv.co.common.x8` access in `ftCo_Rebirth_Cam`.
Review whether the automation/docs files from this PR were intentionally excluded from master or moved elsewhere.
Compare later `ftCh_Init.c` changes for named constants replacing raw motion IDs `0x151` and `0x180`.
